_README: Evaluation fixture for pebbl's memory rerank feature (next slice; NOT implemented here).
One fictional project: 'Lattice', a collaborative whiteboard app.
REAL logs columns (present in v0.5 schema): id, timestamp, source, category, tier, message, topics, corrects, relates_to, valid_to, invalidated_by.
RERANK SIGNALS not yet in the schema (this slice does NOT add the columns): importance, last_accessed, access_count.
FIXTURE-ONLY fields: time_sensitive (boolean guardrail input), relevance (flat lexical match score, kept NEUTRAL across competitors so it does not smuggle the answer).
tier values: foundation > component > detail > fleeting.
valid_to: NULL string means current belief; an ISO timestamp means superseded, and invalidated_by names the entry that replaced it.
expected_top_k in queries[] is COMPUTED by test/rerank-ground-truth.js and frozen here. Do not hand-edit; re-run the deriver.
Adversarial pairs are tagged in each entry's _note for the human auditor.

entries.message: Auth model: short-lived JWT access token plus rotating refresh token in httpOnly cookie
entries._note: PAIR A (auth): OLD ~6mo foundation, HIGH usage. Must outrank fresh fleeting note id 2 despite being older.

entries.message: Tried bumping JWT access token TTL to 30m locally to debug a logout loop, reverted
entries._note: PAIR A (auth): FRESH fleeting, ZERO usage. Recency says it wins; rerank must rank it below foundation id 1.

entries.message: Refresh token rotation handled by an auth service module, not inline in route handlers
entries._note: auth: component, med usage. Sits between foundation and details on auth ordering.

entries.message: Auth cookie SameSite set to Lax so the OAuth redirect callback keeps the session
entries._note: auth: detail, low usage.

entries.message: Password reset tokens expire after 1 hour and are single-use
entries._note: auth: detail, low usage.

entries.message: Realtime sync uses CRDTs (Yjs) so concurrent edits merge without a central lock
entries._note: PAIR A (realtime-sync): OLD ~6mo foundation, HIGH usage. Must outrank fresh fleeting id 7.

entries.message: Skimmed a blog comparing Yjs vs Automerge again, no action taken
entries._note: PAIR A (realtime-sync): FRESH fleeting, ZERO usage. Recency bait; must rank below foundation id 6.

entries.message: Sync transport is a WebSocket relay; updates broadcast per board room
entries._note: realtime-sync: component, med usage.

entries.message: Yjs awareness protocol carries cursor presence; throttled to 50ms per client
entries._note: realtime-sync: detail, low usage.

entries.message: Sync reconnect uses exponential backoff capped at 10s
entries._note: realtime-sync: detail, low usage.

entries.message: Board documents stored in Postgres as a JSONB snapshot plus an append-only update log
entries._note: PAIR B (storage): SUPERSEDED foundation. valid_to set, replaced by id 12. Must be EXCLUDED entirely despite high tier/usage.

entries.message: Board documents stored in S3 as compacted CRDT snapshots; Postgres holds only metadata and pointers
entries._note: PAIR B (storage): CURRENT foundation replacement of id 11. Newer than 11 here so it also covers the simple case; see PAIR B2 (id 13/14) for current-older-than-superseded.

entries.message: Considered moving snapshot compaction to a nightly batch job
entries._note: PAIR B2 (storage): SUPERSEDED component that is RECENT (timestamp 2026-06-10), replaced by the OLDER current decision id 14. Must be excluded; the older current must outrank it.

entries.message: Snapshot compaction runs inline on write once an update log passes 200 ops
entries._note: PAIR B2 (storage): CURRENT component, OLDER (2026-03-12) than the superseded id 13. valid_to NULL. Must rank above the recent-but-superseded id 13 (which is excluded).

entries.message: S3 snapshot keys namespaced by boardId then version; lifecycle rule expires versions older than 90 days
entries._note: storage: detail, low usage, current.

entries.message: Noted S3 egress cost spike during the storage migration week
entries._note: storage: fleeting, zero usage, current.

entries.message: Canvas rendered on HTML5 canvas with a scene graph; no DOM nodes per shape
entries._note: rendering: OLD foundation, HIGH usage, current. Anchor for rendering query.

entries.message: Rendering split into a static layer and a live layer; only the live layer repaints on drag
entries._note: rendering: component, med usage, current.

entries.message: Dirty-rect tracking limits repaint to changed bounding boxes
entries._note: rendering: detail, low usage, current.

entries.message: BUILD BROKEN: rendering layer split landed a regression, shapes vanish on zoom; hotfix in progress
entries._note: PAIR C (rendering/ci): TIME-SENSITIVE recent incident. A detail tier so normal ordering would bury it; the rerank guardrail must keep it visible. Not enforced by the coarse rule here (that is the next slice); flagged for the guardrail test.

entries.message: CI runs on GitHub Actions; merges to main gated on unit plus a Playwright e2e suite
entries._note: ci: OLD foundation, HIGH usage, current.

entries.message: e2e suite sharded into 4 parallel jobs to keep CI under 8 minutes
entries._note: PAIR B3 (ci): SUPERSEDED component, replaced by newer id 23. Must be excluded.

entries.message: e2e suite sharded into 8 parallel jobs after flake rate rose; target CI under 6 minutes
entries._note: PAIR B3 (ci): CURRENT replacement of id 22. Newer and current; must rank above the excluded 22.

entries.message: CI caches pnpm store keyed on lockfile hash
entries._note: ci: detail, low usage, current.

entries.message: CI red: Playwright flake on the share-dialog test, retried green; tracking flakiness
entries._note: ci: detail, low usage, current. Older incident, time_sensitive false (resolved).

entries.message: Public API is REST under /v1 with cursor pagination; GraphQL explicitly out of scope
entries._note: api: OLD foundation, HIGH usage, current.

entries.message: API auth via the same JWT access token; scopes carried as a claim
entries._note: api+auth: component, med usage, current. Cross-topic so it appears under both api and auth queries.

entries.message: API rate limit 100 req/min per token via a sliding-window counter in Redis
entries._note: api: detail, low usage, current.

entries.message: Wondered aloud whether /v1 cursor pagination should expose total counts
entries._note: api: FRESH fleeting, zero usage, current. Recency bait under the api query; must rank below foundation/component.

entries.message: Onboarding is a guided 3-step tour rendered as a real board the user can edit
entries._note: PAIR B4 (onboarding): SUPERSEDED foundation. Replaced by id 31. Must be excluded; replacement is also foundation but newer.

entries.message: Onboarding replaced with a single template-picker screen; the guided tour was dropped as too slow
entries._note: PAIR B4 (onboarding): CURRENT foundation replacement of id 30. Must outrank the excluded 30.

entries.message: Template picker offers 6 starter templates; selection deep-links into a new board
entries._note: onboarding: detail, low usage, current.

entries.message: Idea: add a skip link on the template picker for returning users
entries._note: onboarding: FRESH fleeting, zero usage, current. Recency bait.

entries.message: Onboarding tracks completion as a per-user flag in the metadata table
entries._note: onboarding: component, low-med usage, current.

entries.message: Storage: pointer rows carry a content hash for integrity checks on read
entries._note: storage: detail, low usage, current. Fairly recent.

entries.message: Auth: failed login attempts rate-limited to 5 per 15 minutes per account
entries._note: auth: detail, low usage, current. Higher access than 4/5 to test the recency tiebreak inside the low band.

entries.message: Monorepo with pnpm workspaces; web client, sync server, and shared types are separate packages
entries._note: architecture: OLD foundation, high usage, current. Topic with only a few entries.

entries.message: Shared types package is the single source of truth for board document schemas
entries._note: architecture: component, med usage, current.

entries.message: Thought about extracting a fourth package for the rendering engine
entries._note: architecture: FRESH fleeting, zero usage, current. Recency bait.

entries.message: Architecture: shared types published internally via workspace protocol, not a registry
entries._note: architecture: detail, low usage, current.

entries.message: Realtime-sync: server persists Yjs updates to the storage layer every 5s or 100 ops
entries._note: realtime-sync+storage cross-topic: detail, med-low usage, current. Appears under both queries.

entries.message: BUILD BROKEN: sync server crash-loops on a malformed Yjs update from an old client; rollback staged
entries._note: PAIR C (realtime-sync): TIME-SENSITIVE recent incident, detail tier. Guardrail must keep it surfaced; coarse rule here will rank it within the detail band.

entries.message: Auth sessions list lets a user revoke a device; revocation invalidates that refresh token family
entries._note: auth: OLD component, LOW usage. Tests that a component still beats details, and recency tiebreak vs id 3 (id 3 is newer + higher band).

entries.message: Rendering: text shapes measured off-DOM in a hidden canvas to avoid layout thrash
entries._note: rendering: FRESH detail, low usage, current. Recency tiebreak vs older details in the band.

entries.message: Rendering: viewport culling skips scene-graph nodes outside the visible bounds
entries._note: rendering: component, med usage, current. Recent enough to test recency tiebreak vs id 18 inside the component+med band.

entries.message: API: error envelope is {error:{code,message,requestId}} across all /v1 routes
entries._note: api: detail, low usage, current.

entries.message: API: added an idempotency-key header on board-create POSTs
entries._note: api: FRESH detail, low usage, current. Recency tiebreak inside the detail+low band vs older details.

entries.message: Billing is metered per active board per month; usage is aggregated nightly and the meter is the source of truth for invoices
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (billing): LOAD-BEARING component. Lowest id in the billing component cluster, OLD, HIGH usage (26), importance 5. This is the metering decision every invoice depends on, so it is genuinely the most load-bearing billing belief. tier-then-id (id DESC) buries it at the bottom of the cluster; oracle (usage band) and rerank float it near the top. The conflict here is USAGE, which the live path ignores.

entries.message: Proration on mid-cycle plan changes is computed by day; downgrades credit the next invoice rather than refunding
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (billing): component, MED usage (9), newer than 48. Same tier as 48 so tier cannot order them; usage band puts it below 48 but above the low-usage filler.

entries.message: Failed card charges enter a 3-attempt dunning sequence over 7 days before the workspace is downgraded to free
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (billing): component, MED usage (7).

entries.message: Invoices are generated by Stripe Billing; we mirror the invoice id and status into our own ledger table
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (billing): component, MED usage (6), newer than 49/50.

entries.message: Annual plans get a 2-month discount applied as a coupon at checkout
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (billing): component, LOW usage (2). A real but minor pricing knob; barely referenced. tier-then-id floats it above 48 (higher id); oracle/rerank push it below.

entries.message: Tax handling delegated to Stripe Tax; we store the computed tax line per invoice for reporting
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (billing): component, LOW usage (1), NEWEST and HIGHEST id in the cluster. tier-then-id ranks it FIRST among components; oracle/rerank rank it last. The crux of the conflict.

entries.message: Billing model: per-seat is rejected; we charge per active board so pricing tracks value delivered, not headcount
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (billing): FOUNDATION anchor. Tops both orderings via tier, so the discrimination happens purely among the same-tier components below it.

entries.message: Permission checks resolve through a single can(user, action, resource) gate so every route shares one authorization path
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (permissions): LOAD-BEARING detail. Lowest id, OLD, HIGH usage (21). The shared gate every route calls, so it is the most pulled-up permissions belief despite being a detail tier. tier-then-id (id DESC) buries it; oracle/rerank surface it on usage.

entries.message: Board roles are owner, editor, commenter, viewer; the role grants a fixed action set
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (permissions): detail, MED usage (8).

entries.message: Share links carry a scoped capability token so anonymous viewers never get a full session
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (permissions): detail, MED usage (6).

entries.message: Org admins can list and revoke any board share within their workspace
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (permissions): detail, MED usage (5, at the band floor).

entries.message: Commenter role may add comments but is blocked from moving or deleting shapes
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (permissions): detail, LOW usage (2).

entries.message: Viewer role hides the right-hand properties panel entirely
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (permissions): detail, LOW usage (0), NEWEST and HIGHEST id. tier-then-id ranks it first among the details; oracle/rerank rank it last. A minor UI detail, plausibly the least load-bearing.

entries.message: Authorization is enforced server-side on every mutation; the client UI gating is advisory only
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (permissions): COMPONENT anchor. Tops both orderings via tier; the discrimination happens among the same-tier details below it.

entries.message: Notifications fan out through a single outbox table polled by a worker, so a crash never drops an event
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (notifications): FOUNDATION anchor. Tops both via tier; conflict is among the same-tier components below.

entries.message: Delivery is email plus in-app; per-user channel preferences gate every send and default to in-app only
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (notifications): LOAD-BEARING component. Lowest id, OLD, HIGH usage (19). The preference gate every send consults, so it is the most load-bearing component. tier-then-id buries it; oracle/rerank surface it on usage.

entries.message: Digest mode batches low-priority notifications into a daily summary email
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (notifications): component, MED usage (9).

entries.message: Mentions trigger an immediate notification that bypasses digest batching
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (notifications): component, MED usage (7).

entries.message: Email sends go through SES with a per-workspace suppression list for bounces
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (notifications): component, MED usage (6).

entries.message: Notification rows older than 60 days are pruned by a nightly job
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (notifications): component, LOW usage (2).

entries.message: Added a per-notification dismiss-all control in the in-app tray
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (notifications): component, LOW usage (1), NEWEST and HIGHEST id. tier-then-id ranks it first among components; oracle/rerank rank it last.

entries.message: Board export serializes the CRDT document to a portable JSON the importer can round-trip without loss
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (export): LOAD-BEARING detail. Lowest id, OLD, HIGH usage (23). The serialization format everything else builds on, so the most pulled-up export belief. tier-then-id buries it; oracle/rerank surface it on usage.

entries.message: PNG export rasterizes the current viewport at 2x for retina
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (export): detail, MED usage (9).

entries.message: PDF export paginates large boards by tiling the scene graph across pages
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (export): detail, MED usage (7).

entries.message: Export runs in a web worker so the main thread stays responsive on big boards
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (export): detail, MED usage (6).

entries.message: SVG export inlines fonts so text renders identically off-platform
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (export): detail, LOW usage (2).

entries.message: Added a copy-as-image-to-clipboard shortcut alongside file export
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (export): detail, LOW usage (1), NEWEST and HIGHEST id. tier-then-id ranks it first among the details; oracle/rerank rank it last.

entries.message: Export and import share one schema-versioned format module so a newer export still imports into an older client
entries._note: WITHIN-TIER (export): COMPONENT anchor. Tops both orderings via tier; the discrimination happens among the same-tier details below it.

queries.intent: where and how are board documents stored
what is the public API shape
how does billing and invoicing work
queries._note: WITHIN-TIER: 6 same-tier (component) billing entries + 1 foundation anchor. tier cannot order the 6, and insertion-order (id) CONFLICTS with usage. tier-then-id floats the newest low-usage component (id 53) and buries the load-bearing high-usage one (id 48). Built to defeat tier-then-id via the USAGE band the live path ignores.

queries.intent: what is the pricing and monetization model
queries._note: WITHIN-TIER cross-topic: same billing entries seen via the money tag (ids 48,49,54 are billing,money). Confirms the within-tier conflict is not an artifact of one topic label.

WITHIN-TIER: 6 same-tier (detail) permissions entries + 1 component anchor. Shows the conflict is tier-agnostic: the load-bearing shared-gate detail (id 55, high usage, low id) is buried by tier-then-id and recovered by usage.
WITHIN-TIER: 6 same-tier (component) notifications entries + 1 foundation anchor. Load-bearing preference-gate component (id 63, high usage, low id) buried by tier-then-id, recovered by usage.

queries.intent: how does board export and import work
queries._note: WITHIN-TIER: 6 same-tier (detail) export entries + 1 component anchor. Load-bearing serialization-format detail (id 69, high usage, low id) buried by tier-then-id, recovered by usage.
